statement.en: Collatz drift constants log(3/4), log(3/2), log(2) admit NO simple polynomial continued-fraction identity within the search space (α·i+β, γ·i²+δ·i+ε) tested by Rei-AIOS (8,575 patterns, 0 matches within 1e-4). Sanity check: 2/log(3) matched machine-precision Euler identity in same search. Conjecture: Collatz drift constants are outside the CF-closure of simple integer-polynomial generators, suggesting deeper transcendence or algebraic structure.
statement.ja: Collatz drift 定数 log(3/4), log(3/2), log(2) は simple polynomial 連分数 identity を持たない (8,575 patterns で 0 matches). Sanity check として 2/log(3) は machine-precision で一致. 予想: Collatz drift 定数は simple integer-polynomial CF closure 外, より深い transcendence 構造
statement.latex: \text{No } (\alpha i + \beta, \gamma i^2 + \delta i + \epsilon) \text{ CF identity for } \log(3/4), \log(3/2), \log(2) \text{ within 1e-4 tolerance}

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Negative result in restricted search space
reiTyping.rationale: Informs where NOT to search for Collatz descent identities

formalization.notes: Empirical result; negative findings hard to formalize without exhaustive characterization of CF-closure

known_progress.event: STEP 994 Rei-AIOS ran 8,575 polynomial CF patterns on 8 target constants
known_progress.impact: Positive: 2/log(3) Euler identity rediscovered (err 1.54e-16). Negative: 3 Collatz constants found no match.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Open: extend search space (rational-function coefficients, signed products). STEP 997 candidate.
